name: graphql-architect
description: Master modern GraphQL with federation, performance optimization,
capabilities: Design federated GraphQL schemas with Apollo Federation v2 and subgraph patterns
Implement DataLoader caching and query optimization to eliminate N+1 problems
Configure field-level authorization, rate limiting, and introspection security
Build real-time subscription systems with WebSocket and server-sent events
Create migration strategies from REST APIs to GraphQL with backward compatibility
Generate type-safe clients using GraphQL Code Generator and schema tooling
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines and detected no suspicious patterns. This is a prompt-only skill containing instructional content for GraphQL architecture guidance. No executable code, network access, filesystem operations, or external command execution present.
valueStatement: Building production GraphQL systems requires expertise in federation, performance, and security. This skill provides proven patterns for scalable schema design and optimization.
userTitle: Design scalable GraphQL APIs with enterprise patterns
limitations: Cannot execute code or deploy GraphQL servers directly
Requires manual implementation of suggested schemas and resolvers
Does not replace dedicated GraphQL testing and monitoring tools
Recommendations assume standard GraphQL server environments

useCases.targetUser: Backend architects leading GraphQL adoption across multiple engineering teams
useCases.title: Enterprise Federation Architecture
useCases.description: Design a multi-team federated GraphQL architecture with subgraph composition, schema governance, and cross-team collaboration patterns.

useCases.targetUser: Performance engineers optimizing high-traffic GraphQL APIs
useCases.title: Performance Optimization Audit
useCases.description: Analyze existing GraphQL schemas for N+1 queries, implement DataLoader patterns, and configure multi-level caching strategies.

useCases.targetUser: Security engineers responsible for GraphQL API protection
useCases.title: Security Hardening Implementation
useCases.description: Implement field-level authorization, query complexity analysis, rate limiting, and production security configurations.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Schema Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a GraphQL schema for a [resource type] with CRUD operations. Include appropriate scalar types, input types for mutations, and follow naming conventions. Explain the type relationships.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a new GraphQL API from scratch

promptTemplates.title: Federation Subgraph Planning
promptTemplates.prompt: Plan an Apollo Federation v2 subgraph for [domain]. Identify entities, key directives, and external type references. Show how this subgraph integrates with the overall federated architecture.
promptTemplates.scenario: Distributing GraphQL architecture across multiple teams

promptTemplates.title: Performance Optimization Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this GraphQL schema and resolver implementation for performance issues. Identify N+1 query patterns, suggest DataLoader implementations, and recommend caching strategies at field and query levels.
promptTemplates.scenario: Optimizing an existing GraphQL API for production traffic

promptTemplates.title: Security Configuration Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Audit this GraphQL server configuration for security vulnerabilities. Check introspection settings, CORS configuration, rate limiting, query depth limits, and field-level authorization. Provide specific remediation steps.
promptTemplates.scenario: Hardening a GraphQL API before production deployment

bestPractices: Design schemas with versioning and evolution in mind - use deprecation before removal
Implement DataLoader for all resolver fetching to prevent N+1 query problems
Configure query complexity analysis and rate limiting before production deployment
antiPatterns: Exposing database models directly in GraphQL schema without abstraction layers
Returning unbounded lists without connection patterns or pagination
Implementing business logic in resolvers instead of dedicated service layers

faq.question: What is Apollo Federation and when should I use it?
faq.answer: Apollo Federation enables splitting a GraphQL schema across multiple services. Use it when multiple teams own different domains or when microservices need to expose unified GraphQL APIs.

faq.question: How do I prevent N+1 query problems in GraphQL?
faq.answer: Use DataLoader to batch and cache database requests. DataLoader collects individual requests and executes them as a single batch query, reducing database round trips significantly.

faq.question: What security measures are essential for production GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Implement query depth limiting, query complexity analysis, rate limiting, field-level authorization, disable introspection in production, and configure proper CORS headers.

faq.question: How do I handle authentication in GraphQL resolvers?
faq.answer: Extract authentication from context in your Apollo Server setup. Access user information via context.user in resolvers and implement field-level checks using middleware or directive patterns.

faq.question: What is the difference between queries and mutations?
faq.answer: Queries fetch data and can be cached. Mutations modify data and always execute. Use queries for read operations and mutations for create, update, or delete operations.

faq.question: How do I implement real-time features in GraphQL?
faq.answer: Use GraphQL subscriptions with WebSocket connections. Define subscription types in your schema, implement pubsub mechanisms, and ensure proper authorization and cleanup on disconnect.
